key_insight: In WTaCrV refractory high-entropy alloy, local chemical order and random solid-solution both change irradiation damage pathways, but they help preserve mechanical strength better than the average-atom model.
engineering_relevance: Designing chemical order and composition fluctuations into RHEAs may improve tolerance to reactor irradiation while reducing strength loss under extreme conditions.
method: Atomistic modeling of three alloy states: Average-Atom (AA), random solid-solution (RSS), and local chemical order (LCO).
Collision-cascade simulations to compare primary defect production under identical radiation dose.
Mechanical response assessment after irradiation, including strength and flow stress degradation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports Frenkel-pair production follows NRSS > NLCO > NAA, yet RSS and LCO show limited irradiation-induced mechanical degradation compared with AA.
watch_item: Results depend on the newly developed interatomic potentials and the idealized atomistic configurations, so transferability to real microstructures and long-timescale reactor conditions may be limited.
paper_title: Primary damage and mechanical degradation of WTaCrV refractory high-entropy alloy: effects of solid-solution and chemical ordering
